title: Payroll Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Payroll runs, tax withholding calculations, and standard reporting are largely automatable end-to-end; multi-jurisdiction edge cases and dispute resolution still need a specialist.
rationales.toolMaturity: Payroll platforms with embedded AI compliance and calculation engines are mature and near-universal in the toolchain.
rationales.adoption: Automated payroll processing is a mainstream expectation across nearly every employer size and sector.
rationales.agenticExposure: Payroll is substantially a pipeline of digital decisions — calculate, withhold, remit, report — that an agent can traverse end-to-end for standard cases.

tasksGrowing: Payroll system migration and integration projects
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 43-3051 (Payroll and Timekeeping Clerks), p25-p75 rounded
insight: Standard payroll runs are now close to fully automated by platforms with built-in compliance engines, pushing this role toward High risk. The remaining ground is genuinely hard compliance — multi-state, cross-border, system-migration work — where a mistake is expensive enough that employers still want a specialist.
